*   Comment
*   Published: 03 March 2023

# The end game: respecting major sources of population diversity

*   [Jakub Kopal](#auth-Jakub-Kopal-Aff1-Aff2)  [ORCID: orcid.org/0000-0002-1201-2872](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-1201-2872)[1](#Aff1),[2](#Aff2),
*   [Lucina Q. Uddin](#auth-Lucina_Q_-Uddin-Aff3)  [ORCID: orcid.org/0000-0003-2278-8962](https://orcid.org/0000-0003-2278-8962)[3](#Aff3) &
*   [Danilo Bzdok](#auth-Danilo-Bzdok-Aff1-Aff2-Aff4)  [ORCID: orcid.org/0000-0003-3466-6620](https://orcid.org/0000-0003-3466-6620)[1](#Aff1),[2](#Aff2),[4](#Aff4) 

[*Nature Methods*](/nmeth) **volume 20**, pages 1122–1128 (2023) [Cite this article](#citeas) 

[Save article](/articles/s41592-023-01812-3/save-research?_csrf=lANLeRdbG9lPymzI98TvWph90vy85FBC)

[View saved research](/saved-research) 

*   6253 Accesses

*   64 Citations

*   62 Altmetric

*   [Metrics details](/articles/s41592-023-01812-3/metrics)

### Subjects

*   [Medical research](/subjects/medical-research)
*   [Neuroscience](/subjects/neuroscience)
*   [Scientific community](/subjects/scientific-community)

Human neuroscience is enjoying burgeoning population data resources: large-scale cohorts with thousands of participant profiles of gene expression, brain scanning and sociodemographic measures. The depth of phenotyping puts us in a better position than ever to fully embrace major sources of population diversity as effects of interest to illuminate mechanisms underlying brain health.

[Access through your institution](https://wayf.springernature.com?redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.nature.com%2Farticles%2Fs41592-023-01812-3)

**Fig. 1: Dominance of European participants in GWAS degrades phenotype prediction performance in individuals from non-European communities.**

![](//media.springernature.com/m312/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41592-023-01812-3/MediaObjects/41592_2023_1812_Fig1_HTML.png)

**Fig. 2: Major sources of population stratification are entangled with COVID-19 outcomes across ~10,000 US families.**

![](//media.springernature.com/m312/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41592-023-01812-3/MediaObjects/41592_2023_1812_Fig2_HTML.png)

**Fig. 3: Handedness shapes brain function depending on cultural and geographic diversity.**

![](//media.springernature.com/m312/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41592-023-01812-3/MediaObjects/41592_2023_1812_Fig3_HTML.png)

**Fig. 4: Population diversity is linked to more brittle predictive patterns especially in the higher association cortex.**

![](//media.springernature.com/m312/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41592-023-01812-3/MediaObjects/41592_2023_1812_Fig4_HTML.png)

33.  Raina, P. et al. *Int. J. Epidemiol.* **48**, 1752–1753j (2019).

     [Article](https://doi.org/10.1093%2Fije%2Fdyz173)  [PubMed](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/entrez/query.fcgi?cmd=Retrieve&db=PubMed&dopt=Abstract&list_uids=31633757)  [PubMed Central](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6929533)  [Google Scholar](http://scholar.google.com/scholar_lookup?&title=&journal=Int.%20J.%20Epidemiol.&doi=10.1093%2Fije%2Fdyz173&volume=48&pages=1752-1753j&publication_year=2019&author=Raina%2CP) 

34.  Warnert, E. A. H. et al. *J. Magn. Reson. Imaging* **53**, 1608–1611 (2021).

     [Article](https://doi.org/10.1002%2Fjmri.27476)  [PubMed](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/entrez/query.fcgi?cmd=Retrieve&db=PubMed&dopt=Abstract&list_uids=33350020)  [Google Scholar](http://scholar.google.com/scholar_lookup?&title=&journal=J.%20Magn.%20Reson.%20Imaging&doi=10.1002%2Fjmri.27476&volume=53&pages=1608-1611&publication_year=2021&author=Warnert%2CEAH) 

35.  McManus, I. C. In *Language Lateralization and Psychosis* (eds Sommer, I. E. C. & Kahn, R. S.) 37–57 (Cambridge Univ. Press, 2009).
